message:
fix(lab): restore correct indentation and remove debug println from task polling

The previous commit (28285224 "fix scheduler lock scope") accidentally
introduced deeply-indented code in the task polling section and left the
debug println on the same line as `let result = ...`, breaking both
readability and formatting consistency.

This commit:
- Restores standard 8-space indentation for the "// 4. Poll the task"
  comment and the code block that follows
- Removes the conditional debug println ("Executing {:?} at step ...")
  that was added for development-time tracing — the scheduler already
  has adequate observability through the spectral health and tracing
  subsystems, so this per-step stdout logging is unnecessary noise
- Separates the `let result = ...` assignment back onto its own line
  where it belongs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
